sources.text: On the Halbrook pier project, the concrete pier footings were poured Monday at 08:00. Steel columns are to be set on those footings by crane once the concrete has cured sufficiently to bear the load.
The structural specification requires the footings to cure for 96 hours before any crane loading is applied to them.
The crane is available only on Thursday at 08:00 this week; it is booked elsewhere for every other day, with no alternative slot before next week.

assumption_A.text: The structural specification requires the footings to cure for 96 hours before any crane loading is applied to them.

not_A_evidence.text: Engineering revision: a high-early-strength concrete mix was used, so the required cure time before crane loading is reduced to 48 hours.

question: Will the footings have cured in time for the Thursday 08:00 crane loading, and can the column setting proceed as scheduled?
must_change: The footings will not have cured in time for the Thursday crane loading.
The steel column setting cannot proceed on Thursday as scheduled.
must_persist: The pier footings were poured Monday at 08:00.
The crane is available only on Thursday this week.

sources.text: Refrigerated van Frost-9 runs a rural delivery loop from the Calder depot. The route goes depot to Client Aldrege, then to Client Boswin, then back to the depot, with no refuelling stops available anywhere along the rural route.
The three legs measure 140 km from the depot to Aldrege, 110 km from Aldrege to Boswin, and 95 km from Boswin back to the depot.
Frost-9's engine and reefer unit together give it a single-fill driving range of 300 km before it must refuel.

assumption_A.text: Frost-9's engine and reefer unit together give it a single-fill driving range of 300 km before it must refuel.

not_A_evidence.text: Fleet update: Frost-9 was fitted with an auxiliary tank, raising its single-fill driving range to 400 km.

question: Can Frost-9 complete the full delivery loop to both clients on a single fuel fill, and if not, what is required?
must_change: The delivery loop cannot be completed on a single fuel fill.
A refuelling stop or a route split is required to serve both clients.
must_persist: The depot-to-Aldrege leg is 140 km.
There are no refuelling stops available along the rural route.

sources.text: Corviss Catering must blind-bake 480 tart shells for a 09:30 dispatch. The convection oven Hearth-2 is the only oven assigned to this job, and it becomes available at 08:00.
Hearth-2 holds 6 trays per bake cycle, each tray carries 20 tart shells, and one bake cycle takes 25 minutes.
The shells must all be baked before the 09:30 dispatch time; there is no way to hand any of them off to another oven.

assumption_A.text: Hearth-2 holds 6 trays per bake cycle, each tray carries 20 tart shells, and one bake cycle takes 25 minutes.

not_A_evidence.text: Equipment note: Hearth-2 was retrofitted with a taller rack and now holds 8 trays per bake cycle (still 20 shells per tray, 25 minutes per cycle).

question: How many bake cycles are needed for all 480 tart shells, and can they all be baked before the 09:30 dispatch?
must_change: Four bake cycles are required to blind-bake all 480 tart shells.
The tart shells cannot all be baked before the 09:30 dispatch.
must_persist: The catering order is for 480 tart shells.
Each bake cycle takes 25 minutes.

sources.text: Export container CU-338 is loaded for road haulage from the Tamworth yard. It carries 22 pallets, each weighing 850 kg, and the empty container has a tare weight of 2,300 kg.

The haulage road permit certifies a maximum gross laden weight of 20,000 kg for container CU-338, combining its tare and cargo. A container above the certified weight may not travel on the permitted route.

assumption_A.text: The haulage road permit certifies a maximum gross laden weight of 20,000 kg for container CU-338, combining its tare and cargo.

not_A_evidence.text: Permit amendment: CU-338's road haulage permit was upgraded, certifying a maximum gross laden weight of 22,000 kg.

question: Does the laden container comply with its certified gross weight limit, and if not, what must be done for it to travel?
must_change: The laden container exceeds its certified gross weight limit.
At least two pallets must be removed for the container to be road-legal.
must_persist: Container CU-338 is loaded with 22 pallets weighing 850 kg each.
The empty container has a tare weight of 2,300 kg.

sources.text: The Selby aquatics centre is opening its 50-metre pool for an evening gala. A peak of 180 swimmers is expected in the water at the busiest point of the event.
Safety regulation requires at least one certified lifeguard on deck for every 25 swimmers in the water.
Six certified lifeguards are rostered for the gala, and there are no on-call reserves available that evening.

assumption_A.text: Safety regulation requires at least one certified lifeguard on deck for every 25 swimmers in the water.

not_A_evidence.text: Regulatory update: the on-deck ratio was revised to one certified lifeguard for every 35 swimmers in the water.

question: Are enough certified lifeguards rostered to open the pool at peak attendance, and how many are required?
must_change: Eight certified lifeguards are required for the peak swim load.
The rostered lifeguard staffing is insufficient to open the pool at peak attendance.
must_persist: A peak of 180 swimmers is expected in the water.
Six certified lifeguards are rostered for the gala.

sources.text: Driver Halvard is assigned a three-leg delivery run for a single duty period. The three legs require 3 hours 20 minutes, 2 hours 50 minutes, and 3 hours 30 minutes of driving respectively.
A driving-time cap of 9 hours applies to any single duty period. Driving beyond the cap in one duty period is not permitted.

assumption_A.text: A driving-time cap of 9 hours applies to any single duty period.

not_A_evidence.text: Schedule-rule revision: the driving-time cap for a single duty period was raised to 10 hours.

question: Does the three-leg run fit within the driver's duty-period driving cap, and can one driver complete it in a single duty period?
must_change: The three-leg run exceeds the driver's duty-period driving cap.
The run cannot be completed by a single driver in one duty period.
must_persist: The three legs require 3 hours 20 minutes, 2 hours 50 minutes, and 3 hours 30 minutes of driving.
The run is assigned to driver Halvard.

sources.text: Outbound truck OT-5 at the Rushmere cross-dock is loading for the West region. Pallets on this lane are single-stack only and may not be double-stacked.
The floor of truck OT-5 holds 26 pallet positions.
Four orders are staged for OT-5: Ordway with 8 pallets, Prewitt with 7, Sange with 6, and Tolley with 9.
OT-5 is the only outbound truck to the West region today; the next one does not leave until tomorrow.
The loading dock is free from 06:00, and a full crew is on hand to load OT-5.

assumption_A.text: The floor of truck OT-5 holds 26 pallet positions.

not_A_evidence.text: Fleet swap: a high-cube trailer replaced the original, so OT-5's floor now holds 34 pallet positions.

question: Can all of the staged pallets fit on the single outbound truck OT-5 today, and if not, what must happen?
must_change: All thirty staged pallets cannot fit on the single outbound truck.
At least four pallets must wait for the next day's outbound truck.
must_persist: The four staged orders total thirty pallets.
Pallets on this lane may not be double-stacked.

sources.text: At the Kelby Centre summit, Session A ends at 11:00 in Hall 2, and the panel Session B begins at 11:25 in the same hall. The hall must be changed over between those times.
The AV team needs 30 minutes to reconfigure Hall 2 from a lecture layout to a panel layout.
Catering refreshes the water station in 5 minutes, working in parallel and not on the critical path of the changeover.
Only Hall 2 is available for the panel; the overflow room is booked for a different track.
Doors open to attendees at the session start time, and the panellists are ready to begin at 11:25.

assumption_A.text: The AV team needs 30 minutes to reconfigure Hall 2 from a lecture layout to a panel layout.

not_A_evidence.text: Update: the AV rig was pre-staged, cutting the Hall 2 reconfiguration time to 15 minutes.

question: Can Hall 2 be reconfigured within the changeover window so the panel starts on time, and if not, what is required?
must_change: The Hall 2 AV reconfiguration cannot be completed within the changeover window.
The panel session cannot start on time without additional AV staff or a delay.
must_persist: The panel session is scheduled to start at 11:25 in Hall 2.
The water station refresh takes 5 minutes and runs in parallel.

sources.text: On the Aldous Tower project, the facade crew is scheduled to begin work on Wednesday at 07:00, working from the erected scaffold.
A scaffold safety inspection is valid for 7 days, and crews may work from the scaffold only while the inspection remains valid.
The last scaffold inspection was carried out on the previous Tuesday at 07:00.
No safety inspector is available on Wednesday or before it; the next inspector slot is on Thursday.
The facade materials were delivered and staged on Tuesday, ready for the crew.

assumption_A.text: A scaffold safety inspection is valid for 7 days, and crews may work from the scaffold only while the inspection remains valid.

not_A_evidence.text: Standard revision: scaffold safety inspections are now valid for 14 days rather than 7.

question: Will the scaffold inspection still be valid for the Wednesday 07:00 facade start, and can the crew begin as scheduled?
must_change: The scaffold inspection will have expired before the Wednesday facade start.
The facade crew cannot begin work on Wednesday until a new inspection is passed.
must_persist: The last scaffold inspection was carried out on the previous Tuesday.
The next inspector slot is on Thursday.

sources.text: Electric shuttle Volt-3 runs an evening hotel loop with five stops before returning to the hotel.
On a full charge, Volt-3 has a driving range of 90 km.
The legs of the loop measure 18 km, 22 km, 15 km, 19 km, and 17 km between stops, plus 14 km on the return to the hotel.
Chargers are available only at the hotel; there are none along the route.
One shuttle is assigned to the evening loop.
The loop must be run without a mid-route recharge.

assumption_A.text: On a full charge, Volt-3 has a driving range of 90 km.

not_A_evidence.text: Fleet note: Volt-3 received a battery upgrade, raising its full-charge driving range to 130 km.

question: Can Volt-3 complete the evening loop on a single charge, and if not, what is required?
must_change: The evening loop cannot be completed on a single charge.
A charging stop is required before the shuttle can finish the loop.
must_persist: The six legs of the loop total 105 km.
Chargers are available only at the hotel, with none along the route.

sources.text: The core facility centrifuge Spinix has one booked run today, after which the instrument goes to scheduled service.
Spinix's rotor holds 24 tubes per run.
Three studies submitted samples for the run: Study Marn with 10 tubes, Study Osley with 9, and Study Rew with 8.
After today's run the centrifuge is taken out for scheduled service and is unavailable for a second run.
All submitted tubes require the same spin protocol, so they can be run together, and the booking is 30 minutes.

assumption_A.text: Spinix's rotor holds 24 tubes per run.

not_A_evidence.text: Facility note: a swing-bucket rotor was fitted to Spinix, raising its capacity to 36 tubes per run.

question: Can all of the submitted tubes be spun in the single booked run, and if not, what must happen?
must_change: All twenty-seven sample tubes cannot be spun in the single booked run.
At least three tubes must be deferred to a later run.
must_persist: The three studies submitted twenty-seven tubes in total.
After this run the centrifuge goes to scheduled service.

sources.text: A consolidation is being planned for reefer container RC-12 bound for the port. The container runs a single temperature setpoint for all cargo inside it.
Pharmaceuticals must ship between 2 and 8 degrees Celsius, and RC-12 is set to 5 degrees Celsius, which is within the pharmaceutical range.
The frozen seafood requires minus 18 degrees Celsius to stay compliant.
Both a pharmaceutical pallet and a seafood pallet are staged for loading into RC-12.
There is one container with one setpoint, and the next reefer to the port sails in three days.

assumption_A.text: The frozen seafood requires minus 18 degrees Celsius to stay compliant.

not_A_evidence.text: Product correction: the seafood pallet is a chilled line, not frozen, and stays compliant between 2 and 8 degrees Celsius.

question: Can the seafood pallet ship in the same reefer as the pharmaceutical pallet at the current setpoint, and if not, what must happen?
must_change: The seafood pallet cannot be shipped in the same reefer as the pharmaceutical pallet.
The seafood pallet must wait for a separate reefer or a different setpoint.
must_persist: Reefer container RC-12 is set to 5 degrees Celsius.
The next reefer to the port sails in three days.

sources.text: The fabrication shop's night shift is running the Brennan contract tonight.
Code requires at least 3 certified pressure-vessel welders to be present whenever pressure-vessel welding is performed.
Tonight's roster has 2 certified pressure-vessel welders scheduled.
The Brennan job includes pressure-vessel welding tonight.
No additional certified welders are on call, and the other fabricators on shift are not certified for pressure-vessel welds.

assumption_A.text: Code requires at least 3 certified pressure-vessel welders to be present whenever pressure-vessel welding is performed.

not_A_evidence.text: Code interpretation update: the minimum for pressure-vessel welding was lowered to 2 certified welders present.

question: Can pressure-vessel welding run on tonight's shift as staffed, and if not, what is required?
must_change: Pressure-vessel welding cannot run on tonight's shift.
At least one more certified pressure-vessel welder must be added for the welding to proceed.
must_persist: Two certified pressure-vessel welders are scheduled for tonight.
The Brennan job includes pressure-vessel welding tonight.

sources.text: A transformer is to be moved by heavy transport from Yard E to Substation N. The only route between them crosses the Calderbrook bridge.
The Calderbrook bridge is posted with a limit of 40 tonnes gross vehicle weight.
The rig consists of a 9-tonne tractor, a 12-tonne trailer, and the 22-tonne transformer.
There is no alternative crossing within 60 km of the route.
The move is scheduled for Saturday night when traffic is lowest.
A permit has been filed for the Saturday move.

assumption_A.text: The Calderbrook bridge is posted with a limit of 40 tonnes gross vehicle weight.

not_A_evidence.text: Bridge notice: after a structural retrofit, the Calderbrook bridge was re-rated to a limit of 45 tonnes gross vehicle weight.

question: Can the loaded rig cross the Calderbrook bridge within its posted limit, and if not, what must change?
must_change: The loaded rig exceeds the Calderbrook bridge weight limit.
The transformer cannot be moved over the Calderbrook bridge on the current rig.
must_persist: The rig's gross weight is 43 tonnes.
There is no alternative crossing within 60 km.

sources.text: In the high bay at the Girton warehouse, the SKU Girder-cap is stored at a height of 7.2 metres. Today's pick list requires retrieving Girder-cap.
The reach truck Lift-R has a maximum lift height of 6.5 metres.
Only Lift-R is assigned to the high bay today; no other truck can service that bay.

assumption_A.text: The reach truck Lift-R has a maximum lift height of 6.5 metres.

not_A_evidence.text: Maintenance note: a mast extension was fitted to Lift-R, raising its maximum lift height to 8.0 metres.

question: Can the reach truck retrieve Girder-cap from its storage location, and if not, what is required?
must_change: The reach truck cannot retrieve the SKU from the 7.2-metre location.
A taller reach truck is required to complete the pick.
must_persist: The SKU Girder-cap is stored at a height of 7.2 metres.
Only Lift-R is assigned to the high bay today.

sources.text: A gala at Foxglove Hall expects 520 guests.
A survey of similar events shows guests arrive at an average of 1.6 guests per car, so about 325 cars are expected.
The on-site lot at Foxglove Hall holds 300 cars.
No overflow lot has been arranged, and valet stacking is not permitted on the property.

assumption_A.text: The on-site lot at Foxglove Hall holds 300 cars.

not_A_evidence.text: Venue update: an adjacent field was opened for the gala, so the available parking now holds 400 cars.

question: Can the parking accommodate all of the expected guest cars, and if not, roughly how many cars are unplaced?
must_change: The parking cannot accommodate all of the expected guest cars.
About twenty-five cars will have no on-site space.
must_persist: About 325 guest cars are expected at the gala.
No overflow lot has been arranged.

sources.text: On the Larkspur site, a precast panel weighing 6.5 tonnes must be set at a 30-metre radius from the tower crane.
The tower crane's load chart gives a rated capacity of 5.5 tonnes at a 30-metre radius.
Only this crane is on site, and the panel must be set from the crane's current position.
The rigging weighs 0.3 tonnes and is added to the load on the hook.
The panel has been delivered and staged next to the setting location.

assumption_A.text: The tower crane's load chart gives a rated capacity of 5.5 tonnes at a 30-metre radius.

not_A_evidence.text: Crane update: a heavier counterweight was fitted, raising the rated capacity to 7.5 tonnes at a 30-metre radius.

question: Can the panel be lifted at the 30-metre radius on the current crane, and if not, what must change?
must_change: The panel cannot be lifted at the 30-metre radius on the current crane.
The crane must be reconfigured or repositioned to set the panel.
must_persist: The precast panel weighs 6.5 tonnes.
The rigging adds 0.3 tonnes to the load.

sources.text: Banquet prep produces 84 sheet trays of chilled canapes that must be held overnight until service the next day.
The walk-in cooler Frostgate holds 60 sheet trays.
There is no second cooler, and the canapes must be held chilled until service.
The prep is completed the night before the banquet.
Ambient holding is not food-safe for these canape items.

assumption_A.text: The walk-in cooler Frostgate holds 60 sheet trays.

not_A_evidence.text: Facilities update: a second walk-in cooler was brought online, raising the combined chilled holding capacity to 96 sheet trays.

question: Can all 84 sheet trays be held chilled overnight, and if not, roughly how many have no space?
must_change: All 84 sheet trays cannot be held chilled overnight.
About twenty-four trays have no chilled holding space.
must_persist: The banquet prep produces 84 sheet trays of canapes.
Ambient holding is not food-safe for these items.

sources.text: A teaching-lab session runs 22 students, and each student needs a fume-hood station for the synthesis step.
The lab has 16 certified fume-hood stations.
The session is a single block, and no second room is available to split the group.

assumption_A.text: The lab has 16 certified fume-hood stations.

not_A_evidence.text: Facilities note: an adjoining hood bank was recommissioned, raising the lab to 28 certified fume-hood stations.

question: Can all 22 students work at a fume-hood station simultaneously, and if not, what must happen?
must_change: Not all 22 students can work at a fume-hood station simultaneously.
At least six students must wait or work in shifts.
must_persist: The session has 22 students needing a fume-hood station.
No second room is available for the session.

sources.text: By the 18:00 carrier pickup, 3,600 parcels must be sorted and out the door at the Everden fulfilment centre.
The sorter line starts running at 15:00.
The sorter processes 800 parcels per hour.
There is one sorter line, and no manual overflow lane is staffed today.
The 18:00 carrier pickup is firm, and all 3,600 parcels are staged and ready to feed the line.

assumption_A.text: The sorter processes 800 parcels per hour.

not_A_evidence.text: Operations update: a second belt was engaged on the sorter, raising throughput to 1,400 parcels per hour.

question: Can all 3,600 parcels be sorted before the 18:00 carrier pickup, and if not, roughly how many miss it?
must_change: Not all 3,600 parcels can be sorted before the 18:00 carrier pickup.
About 1,200 parcels will miss the carrier pickup.
must_persist: 3,600 parcels must be ready for the 18:00 carrier pickup.
The sorter line starts running at 15:00.

sources.text: Editorial schedule: The Marlowe Quarterly print issue must ship to the printer as a finished file on a fixed date. Copyediting of the full manuscript takes 15 working days and cannot begin until the manuscript is handed off from the authors.
Author handoff: The authors will hand off the complete manuscript on working day 0 of the production window.
Printer contract: The finished file is due at the printer on working day 40, and layout, which follows copyediting, always takes exactly 10 working days once copyediting is complete.

assumption_A.text: Copyediting of the full manuscript takes 15 working days and cannot begin until the manuscript is handed off from the authors.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised editorial schedule (supersedes the original editorial schedule): copyediting of the full manuscript now takes 22 working days.

question: On what working day does copyediting finish, and does the schedule meet the printer's working-day-40 deadline?
must_change: Copyediting finishes on working day 15.
Layout finishes on working day 25.
must_persist: The finished file is due at the printer on working day 40.
Layout takes 10 working days once copyediting is complete.

sources.text: Trial logistics memo A: The Halvern Study ships investigational kits to enrolled sites. Each site requires 6 kits per enrolled patient for the full protocol.
Trial logistics memo B: The Nordfield site has 25 patients enrolled and no kits currently on hand.
Trial logistics memo C: The central depot can release at most 120 kits to any single site in one shipment; larger allocations must be split across multiple shipments.

assumption_A.text: Each site requires 6 kits per enrolled patient for the full protocol.

not_A_evidence.text: Protocol amendment notice (supersedes trial logistics memo A): each site now requires 4 kits per enrolled patient for the full protocol.

question: How many kits does the Nordfield site need in total, and can that quantity be delivered in a single shipment under the depot's per-shipment limit?
must_change: The Nordfield site needs 150 kits in total.
The Nordfield allocation takes 2 shipments.
must_persist: The Nordfield site has 25 patients enrolled.
The depot releases at most 120 kits to a single site in one shipment.

sources.text: Tender summary: Vendor Calderon Systems bid a unit price of $320 per workstation in the office fit-out tender.
Tender summary: Vendor Rooksby Interiors bid a firm total of $58,000 for the same 200-workstation scope, with no separate line items.
Tender rules: The contract is awarded to whichever vendor's total for the full 200-workstation scope is lower. Calderon's total is its unit price multiplied by the 200 workstations.

assumption_A.text: Vendor Calderon Systems bid a unit price of $320 per workstation in the office fit-out tender.

not_A_evidence.text: Bid correction (supersedes Calderon's tender summary): Calderon Systems has revised its unit price to $270 per workstation.

question: What is Calderon's total for the full 200-workstation scope, and which vendor wins the award under the lower-total rule?
must_change: Calderon's total for the 200-workstation scope is $64,000.
Rooksby Interiors wins the award as the lower-total vendor.
must_persist: Rooksby Interiors bid a firm total of $58,000 for the 200-workstation scope.
The award goes to the vendor with the lower total for the full scope.

sources.text: Leasing brief: The Tarn Street retail unit is offered at a headline rent of $45 per square foot per year.
Leasing brief: The unit measures 1,200 square feet of leasable area.
Tenant budget note: The prospective tenant, Ferro Cafe, has set an annual rent ceiling of $50,000 and will only proceed if the annual rent falls at or below that ceiling.
Leasing brief: Rent is billed annually as the headline rent per square foot multiplied by the leasable area, with no other charges in the first year.

assumption_A.text: The Tarn Street retail unit is offered at a headline rent of $45 per square foot per year.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised leasing brief (supersedes the headline-rent brief): the Tarn Street unit's headline rent is now set at $38 per square foot per year.

question: What is the annual rent for the Tarn Street unit, and does it fall within Ferro Cafe's $50,000 ceiling?
must_change: The annual rent for the Tarn Street unit is $54,000.
The annual rent for the Tarn Street unit is $4,000 above Ferro Cafe's ceiling.
must_persist: The Tarn Street unit measures 1,200 square feet of leasable area.
Ferro Cafe's annual rent ceiling is $50,000.

sources.text: Energy contract sheet: Under the current supply agreement, the Dellmoor plant pays a fixed rate of $0.14 per kilowatt-hour for grid electricity.
Operations note: The Dellmoor plant consumes 60,000 kilowatt-hours of grid electricity per month.
Finance note: The plant's monthly electricity budget is $9,000. Spend above the budget must be escalated to the finance committee.
Reference: Monthly electricity cost equals the per-kilowatt-hour rate multiplied by monthly consumption.

assumption_A.text: Under the current supply agreement, the Dellmoor plant pays a fixed rate of $0.14 per kilowatt-hour for grid electricity.

not_A_evidence.text: Tariff revision notice (supersedes the energy contract sheet): the Dellmoor plant's grid electricity rate has risen to $0.17 per kilowatt-hour.

question: What is the Dellmoor plant's monthly electricity cost, and does it stay within the $9,000 monthly budget?
must_change: The Dellmoor plant's monthly electricity cost is $8,400.
The Dellmoor plant's monthly electricity cost is $600 under the $9,000 budget.
must_persist: The Dellmoor plant consumes 60,000 kilowatt-hours of grid electricity per month.
The plant's monthly electricity budget is $9,000.

sources.text: Warehouse memo 1: The Ashcombe distribution center picks orders at a sustained rate of 90 orders per hour per active picking lane.
Warehouse memo 2: There are 4 active picking lanes at Ashcombe during the evening shift.
Warehouse memo 3: The evening backlog stands at 2,160 orders and must be cleared within the 6-hour evening shift.
Warehouse memo 4: Total picking capacity for a shift equals the per-lane rate times the number of active lanes times the shift length in hours.

assumption_A.text: The Ashcombe distribution center picks orders at a sustained rate of 90 orders per hour per active picking lane.

not_A_evidence.text: Rate revision (supersedes warehouse memo 1): the sustained picking rate at Ashcombe is now 75 orders per hour per active picking lane.

question: What is Ashcombe's total picking capacity for the 6-hour evening shift, and can the 2,160-order backlog be cleared within the shift?
must_change: Ashcombe's total picking capacity for the evening shift is 2,160 orders.
Each active picking lane clears 540 orders over the evening shift.
must_persist: There are 4 active picking lanes at Ashcombe during the evening shift.
The evening backlog is 2,160 orders.

sources.text: Grant memo 1: The Sorrel Foundation reimburses travel at $0.55 per mile driven for approved field visits.
Grant memo 2: Researcher Nwora logged 800 approved field-visit miles this quarter.
Grant memo 3: A single quarterly travel reimbursement claim above $400 requires the program director's countersignature before payment.

assumption_A.text: The Sorrel Foundation reimburses travel at $0.55 per mile driven for approved field visits.

not_A_evidence.text: Reimbursement policy update (supersedes grant memo 1): the Sorrel Foundation now reimburses approved travel at $0.48 per mile driven.

question: What is Nwora's quarterly travel reimbursement, and does the claim require the program director's countersignature?
must_change: Nwora's quarterly travel reimbursement is $440.
Nwora's reimbursement claim requires the program director's countersignature.
must_persist: Researcher Nwora logged 800 approved field-visit miles this quarter.
A quarterly travel reimbursement claim above $400 requires the program director's countersignature.

sources.text: Print spec: The Halloway atlas is bound in signatures. Each signature holds 16 printed pages.
Print spec: The atlas has 320 printed pages in total.
Bindery quote: The bindery charges a flat $8 for every signature bound, with no other binding charges.
Budget note: The binding budget for the atlas is $150. A binding cost above the budget must be re-quoted.
Reference: The number of signatures equals the total page count divided by the pages per signature; total binding cost equals the number of signatures times the per-signature charge.

assumption_A.text: Each signature holds 16 printed pages.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised print spec (supersedes the signature page spec): each signature now holds 32 printed pages.

question: What is the total binding cost for the Halloway atlas, and does it stay within the $150 binding budget?
must_change: The Halloway atlas requires 20 signatures.
The total binding cost for the Halloway atlas is $160.
must_persist: The Halloway atlas has 320 printed pages in total.
The bindery charges $8 for every signature bound.

sources.text: Customs sheet: The imported Merrow textile lot is dutiable at 6 percent of its declared value.
Customs sheet: The declared value of the Merrow lot is $50,000.
Broker rule: Any single shipment whose assessed duty exceeds $2,500 must have a formal broker filing prepared before release.
Reference: The assessed duty equals the duty rate multiplied by the declared value.
Logistics note: The Merrow lot arrives at the East Quay terminal and is held pending duty assessment.

assumption_A.text: The imported Merrow textile lot is dutiable at 6 percent of its declared value.

not_A_evidence.text: Tariff reclassification (supersedes the customs duty sheet): the Merrow textile lot is now dutiable at 4 percent of its declared value.

question: What is the assessed duty on the Merrow lot, and does it require a formal broker filing before release?
must_change: The assessed duty on the Merrow lot is $3,000.
The Merrow lot requires a formal broker filing before release.
must_persist: The declared value of the Merrow lot is $50,000.
A shipment whose assessed duty exceeds $2,500 must have a formal broker filing prepared.

sources.text: Catering plan: The Everton reception will be served a plated menu of 3 servings per guest.
Catering plan: The reception has 140 confirmed guests.
Kitchen note: Each transport tray holds 60 servings. Servings are prepared in whole trays, so a partly filled tray still counts as one tray.

assumption_A.text: The Everton reception will be served a plated menu of 3 servings per guest.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised catering plan (supersedes the plated-menu plan): the Everton reception will be served 2 servings per guest.

question: How many servings are needed for the Everton reception, and how many transport trays must be prepared?
must_change: The Everton reception needs 420 servings.
The kitchen must prepare 7 transport trays.
must_persist: The Everton reception has 140 confirmed guests.
Each transport tray holds 60 servings.

sources.text: Transit note: On the Riverline route each bus seats 55 commuters and runs full at peak.
Transit note: During the peak hour a single bus completes 4 round trips.
Demand note: The peak-hour demand on the Riverline route is 660 commuters who must all be carried.
Reference: A bus's peak-hour carrying capacity equals its seating capacity times the number of round trips it completes in the hour.
Fleet note: Buses are assigned in whole units; a fractional bus requirement is rounded up to the next whole bus.

assumption_A.text: On the Riverline route each bus seats 55 commuters and runs full at peak.

not_A_evidence.text: Fleet reassignment (supersedes the Riverline seating note): the buses now assigned to the Riverline route seat 44 commuters each.

question: What is each bus's peak-hour carrying capacity on the Riverline route, and how many buses are needed to carry the 660 peak-hour commuters?
must_change: Each bus's peak-hour carrying capacity on the Riverline route is 220 commuters.
Three buses are needed to carry the 660 peak-hour commuters.
must_persist: During the peak hour a single bus completes 4 round trips.
The peak-hour demand on the Riverline route is 660 commuters.

sources.text: Production note: On the Larkfield shoot, each scene requires 2 hours of camera and lighting setup.
Production note: The Larkfield shoot has 18 scenes to capture.
Schedule note: A shooting day provides 12 usable setup-hours, and the shoot is booked for a 3-day schedule. Any overflow beyond the booked days would push the shoot over schedule.

assumption_A.text: On the Larkfield shoot, each scene requires 2 hours of camera and lighting setup.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised production note (supersedes the per-scene setup note): each scene on the Larkfield shoot now requires 3 hours of camera and lighting setup.

question: How many total setup-hours does the Larkfield shoot require, and does it fit within the booked 3-day schedule?
must_change: The Larkfield shoot requires 36 total setup-hours.
The Larkfield shoot needs 3 shooting days.
must_persist: The Larkfield shoot has 18 scenes to capture.
A shooting day provides 12 usable setup-hours.

sources.text: Agronomy report: The Wenlock estate's wheat fields yield 4 tonnes per hectare this season.
Estate record: The Wenlock estate has 90 hectares under wheat.
Contract note: Wenlock has a delivery contract requiring 350 tonnes of wheat this season; a harvest below 350 tonnes fails to meet the contract.
Storage note: The estate's grain silo can hold up to 500 tonnes.
Reference: Total harvest equals the per-hectare yield multiplied by the number of hectares under wheat.

assumption_A.text: The Wenlock estate's wheat fields yield 4 tonnes per hectare this season.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised agronomy report (supersedes the yield report): the Wenlock estate's wheat fields yield 3 tonnes per hectare this season.

question: What is the Wenlock estate's total wheat harvest this season, and does it meet the 350-tonne delivery contract?
must_change: The Wenlock estate's total wheat harvest is 360 tonnes.
The Wenlock silo holds 140 tonnes of spare capacity after the harvest.
must_persist: The Wenlock estate has 90 hectares under wheat.
The delivery contract requires 350 tonnes of wheat this season.

sources.text: Metrics memo: The Cordell app loses 5 percent of its starting customers to churn each month.
Metrics memo: Cordell begins the month with 8,000 customers and adds 500 new customers during the month.
Reference: The month-end customer count equals the starting count minus churned customers plus new customers; churned customers equal the churn rate applied to the starting count.

assumption_A.text: The Cordell app loses 5 percent of its starting customers to churn each month.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised metrics memo (supersedes the churn memo): the Cordell app now loses 8 percent of its starting customers to churn each month.

question: How many customers does Cordell churn during the month, and what is the month-end customer count?
must_change: Cordell churns 400 customers during the month.
Cordell's month-end customer count is 8,100.
must_persist: Cordell begins the month with 8,000 customers.
Cordell adds 500 new customers during the month.

sources.text: Bid sheet: Vendor Pallin Supply quotes a delivered price of $12 per unit, freight included, for the fastener order.
Bid sheet: Vendor Quist Hardware quotes $10 per unit plus a single flat freight charge of $2,400 for the same order.
Order note: The fastener order is for 1,000 units.
Award rule: The order is awarded to whichever vendor's total delivered cost for the full 1,000-unit order is lower.
Reference: Pallin's total is its delivered per-unit price times the order quantity; Quist's total is its per-unit price times the order quantity plus its flat freight charge.

assumption_A.text: Vendor Pallin Supply quotes a delivered price of $12 per unit, freight included, for the fastener order.

not_A_evidence.text: Bid revision (supersedes Pallin's bid sheet): Pallin Supply now quotes a delivered price of $14 per unit, freight included.

question: What is Pallin's total delivered cost for the 1,000-unit order, and which vendor is awarded the order under the lower-total rule?
must_change: Pallin's total delivered cost for the order is $12,000.
Pallin Supply is awarded the order as the lower-total vendor.
must_persist: Quist Hardware quotes $10 per unit plus a flat freight charge of $2,400.
The fastener order is for 1,000 units.

sources.text: Leasing report: The Brackwater apartment block is running at 85 percent occupancy across its 200 units.
Leasing report: Each occupied unit pays $900 in rent per month; vacant units pay nothing.
Finance note: The block's operating cost is $160,000 per month. Rent revenue is collected only on occupied units, at the monthly rent times the number of occupied units.

assumption_A.text: The Brackwater apartment block is running at 85 percent occupancy across its 200 units.

not_A_evidence.text: Updated leasing report (supersedes the occupancy report): the Brackwater block is now running at 92 percent occupancy across its 200 units.

question: How many Brackwater units are occupied, what is the monthly rent revenue, and does it cover the $160,000 monthly operating cost?
must_change: The Brackwater block has 170 occupied units.
The Brackwater block's monthly rent revenue is $153,000.
must_persist: The Brackwater block has 200 units in total.
Each occupied unit pays $900 in rent per month.

sources.text: Staffing memo 1: On the Aldergate ward each nurse is assigned to cover 4 patients per shift.
Staffing memo 2: The Aldergate ward has 52 patients on the day shift.
Staffing memo 3: There are 15 nurses rostered for the day shift.
Staffing memo 4: A shift is adequately staffed only if the number of nurses rostered is at least the number required by the coverage ratio.
Reference: The number of nurses required equals the patient count divided by the patients each nurse covers, rounded up to a whole nurse.

assumption_A.text: On the Aldergate ward each nurse is assigned to cover 4 patients per shift.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised staffing memo (supersedes the coverage-ratio memo): on the Aldergate ward each nurse is now assigned to cover 3 patients per shift.

question: How many nurses does the Aldergate day shift require, and are the 15 rostered nurses adequate?
must_change: The Aldergate day shift requires 13 nurses.
The 15 rostered nurses adequately staff the day shift.
must_persist: The Aldergate ward has 52 patients on the day shift.
There are 15 nurses rostered for the day shift.

sources.text: Box-office note: For the Thornbury concert, ticketing assumes a 10 percent no-show rate among ticket holders.
Box-office note: The Thornbury concert has sold 900 tickets.
Venue note: The Thornbury hall seats 850. Expected attendance is the tickets sold reduced by the no-show rate; attendance above the seating capacity would cause overcrowding.

assumption_A.text: For the Thornbury concert, ticketing assumes a 10 percent no-show rate among ticket holders.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised box-office note (supersedes the no-show assumption): the Thornbury concert now assumes a 5 percent no-show rate among ticket holders.

question: What is the expected attendance at the Thornbury concert, and does it stay within the hall's 850-seat capacity?
must_change: The expected attendance at the Thornbury concert is 810.
The Thornbury concert leaves 40 seats empty.
must_persist: The Thornbury concert has sold 900 tickets.
The Thornbury hall seats 850.

sources.text: Brewhouse note 1: At the Fenwick brewery each production batch yields 240 bottles of finished ale.
Brewhouse note 2: The current run is scheduled for 12 production batches.
Sales note: A distributor contract requires 3,000 bottles from this run; a run that yields fewer than 3,000 bottles fails to meet the contract.
Warehouse note: The finished-goods store can hold up to 4,000 bottles.
Reference: The run's total yield equals the per-batch yield multiplied by the number of batches in the run.

assumption_A.text: At the Fenwick brewery each production batch yields 240 bottles of finished ale.

not_A_evidence.text: Revised brewhouse note (supersedes the per-batch yield note): each production batch at the Fenwick brewery now yields 260 bottles of finished ale.

question: How many bottles does the 12-batch run yield, and does it meet the distributor's 3,000-bottle contract?
must_change: The 12-batch run yields 2,880 bottles.
The run is 120 bottles below the 3,000-bottle contract.
must_persist: The current run is scheduled for 12 production batches.
The distributor contract requires 3,000 bottles from this run.

sources.text: Policy summary: The Harrowgate property policy carries a deductible of $2,500 per claim.
Claim file: The submitted storm-damage claim is assessed at $9,000 in covered loss.
Policy summary: The payout on a single claim equals the assessed covered loss minus the deductible, and any single payout is capped at $7,000; a computed payout above the cap is paid at the cap.

assumption_A.text: The Harrowgate property policy carries a deductible of $2,500 per claim.

not_A_evidence.text: Endorsement notice (supersedes the deductible summary): the Harrowgate property policy deductible is reduced to $1,500 per claim.

question: What is the computed payout on the storm-damage claim, and is it within the $7,000 single-claim cap?
must_change: The computed payout on the storm-damage claim is $6,500.
The computed payout is $500 below the $7,000 single-claim cap.
must_persist: The storm-damage claim is assessed at $9,000 in covered loss.
Any single payout is capped at $7,000.

sources.text: Teamforge sells its collaboration app on two tiers, Basic and Plus, and reports monthly recurring revenue to its investors.
The Basic tier is currently active on 250 seats across all customers.
The Plus tier is currently active on 90 seats across all customers.
Basic is priced at $12 per seat per month; Plus is priced at $40 per seat per month.
Investors requested the Basic-tier monthly revenue and the combined monthly recurring revenue across both tiers.

assumption_A.text: The Basic tier is currently active on 250 seats across all customers.

not_A_evidence.text: Seat-count correction: the Basic tier is active on 310 seats, not 250; the earlier count was understated.

question: What is the Basic-tier monthly revenue, and what is the combined monthly recurring revenue across both tiers?
must_change: The Basic-tier monthly revenue is $3,000.
The combined monthly recurring revenue across both tiers is $6,600.
must_persist: The Plus tier is active on 90 seats.
Plus is priced at $40 per seat per month.

sources.text: Ironvale Works runs a single production line that starts 5,000 units of the Model-C bracket per shift.
The line's good-yield rate is 92 percent; the remaining units are scrapped.
Each good bracket sells for $15.
A fixed rework subsidy of $500 per shift is credited to the line by corporate, independent of output.
Operations wants the count of good brackets per shift and the total sellable revenue per shift including the subsidy.

assumption_A.text: The line's good-yield rate is 92 percent; the remaining units are scrapped.

not_A_evidence.text: Quality revision: the good-yield rate on the Model-C line has dropped to 85 percent, replacing the 92 percent figure.

question: How many good brackets are produced per shift, and what is the total sellable revenue per shift including the subsidy?
must_change: The number of good brackets produced per shift is 4,600.
The total sellable revenue per shift including the subsidy is $69,500.
must_persist: Each good bracket sells for $15.
The fixed rework subsidy per shift is $500.

sources.text: Stonehaul Logistics is quoting a dedicated run of 800 miles for a single customer.
The base linehaul charge for this run is a fixed $1,200, independent of fuel.
A fuel surcharge of $0.30 per mile is added on top of the linehaul charge.
A fixed accessorial fee of $175 covers loading and detention for the run.
The customer asked for the fuel-surcharge cost alone and the all-in quoted price for the run.

assumption_A.text: A fuel surcharge of $0.30 per mile is added on top of the linehaul charge.

not_A_evidence.text: Rate update: the fuel surcharge for this run is $0.45 per mile, superseding the $0.30 figure.

question: What is the fuel-surcharge cost for the run, and what is the all-in quoted price?
must_change: The fuel-surcharge cost for the run is $240.
The all-in quoted price for the run is $1,615.
must_persist: The base linehaul charge for the run is a fixed $1,200.
The fixed accessorial fee for the run is $175.

sources.text: The Larkspur Fund is budgeting a residential fellowship cohort for the coming year.
The cohort will admit 40 scholars this year.
Each scholar's tuition is budgeted at $1,500.
A fixed venue rental of $3,000 and a fixed administrative allocation of $4,500 apply to the cohort regardless of size.
The board requested the total tuition line and the full cohort budget including venue and administration.

assumption_A.text: The cohort will admit 40 scholars this year.

not_A_evidence.text: Admissions revision: the cohort will admit 55 scholars this year, not 40; the intake target was raised.

question: What is the total tuition budget for the cohort, and what is the full cohort budget including venue and administration?
must_change: The total tuition budget for the cohort is $60,000.
The full cohort budget including venue and administration is $67,500.
must_persist: Each scholar's tuition is budgeted at $1,500.
The fixed administrative allocation for the cohort is $4,500.

sources.text: Datapoint API served 2,000,000 billable requests to a customer last month.
Requests are billed at $0.0004 each.
A fixed egress allocation of $120 is charged monthly regardless of request volume.
A fixed support-plan fee of $99 is charged monthly.
The customer wants the request-based charge and the total monthly bill including egress and support.

assumption_A.text: Requests are billed at $0.0004 each.

not_A_evidence.text: Pricing notice: the per-request rate has increased to $0.0006 each, effective for last month's usage.

question: What is the request-based charge for the month, and what is the total monthly bill including egress and support?
must_change: The request-based charge for the month is $800.
The total monthly bill including egress and support is $1,019.
must_persist: The fixed egress allocation is $120 per month.
The fixed support-plan fee is $99 per month.

sources.text: Portside Imports is costing a shipment of specialty parts invoiced in euros.
The goods are invoiced at 3,000 euros.
The applicable exchange rate is 1.10 US dollars per euro.
US-side freight of $450 and a customs duty of $200 apply to the shipment as fixed dollar amounts.
Accounting wants the dollar value of the goods and the total landed cost in dollars including freight and duty.

assumption_A.text: The applicable exchange rate is 1.10 US dollars per euro.

not_A_evidence.text: Treasury update: the applicable exchange rate is now 1.25 US dollars per euro, replacing the 1.10 figure.

question: What is the dollar value of the goods, and what is the total landed cost in dollars including freight and duty?
must_change: The dollar value of the goods is $3,300.
The total landed cost in dollars including freight and duty is $3,950.
must_persist: US-side freight for the shipment is $450.
The customs duty for the shipment is $200.

sources.text: Willowmere Events is costing a reception for 200 confirmed guests.
Beverage service is priced at $6 per guest.
Food is provided as fixed platters costing $2,000 in total, independent of headcount.
Furniture and linen rental is a fixed $800 for the reception.
The client asked for the beverage line cost and the full reception cost including food and rental.

assumption_A.text: Beverage service is priced at $6 per guest.

not_A_evidence.text: Menu update: beverage service is now priced at $9 per guest, superseding the $6 figure.

question: What is the beverage line cost for the reception, and what is the full reception cost including food and rental?
must_change: The beverage line cost for the reception is $1,200.
The full reception cost including food and rental is $4,000.
must_persist: Food platters cost $2,000 in total for the reception.
Furniture and linen rental is a fixed $800.

sources.text: Coastline Media is planning a launch campaign across a click-based channel and two fixed-fee placements.
The click-based channel is expected to deliver 8,000 clicks.
The click-based channel costs $0.75 per click.
A newsletter sponsorship costs a fixed $1,500 and a creative-production fee is a fixed $600, both independent of clicks.
The planner wants the click-channel spend and the total campaign spend including the sponsorship and creative fees.

assumption_A.text: The click-based channel costs $0.75 per click.

not_A_evidence.text: Bid update: the click-based channel now costs $1.00 per click, replacing the $0.75 figure.

question: What is the click-channel spend, and what is the total campaign spend including sponsorship and creative fees?
must_change: The click-channel spend is $6,000.
The total campaign spend including sponsorship and creative fees is $8,100.
must_persist: The newsletter sponsorship costs a fixed $1,500.
The creative-production fee is a fixed $600.

sources.text: Thornbrook Estate is projecting proceeds from its 120-acre wheat block this season.
The expected yield is 3.5 tonnes per acre.
Wheat sells at $200 per tonne under the estate's contract.
A fixed harvest and haulage cost of $5,000 is deducted from the block's proceeds.
The manager wants the total tonnage from the block and the net proceeds after the harvest and haulage deduction.

assumption_A.text: The expected yield is 3.5 tonnes per acre.

not_A_evidence.text: Agronomy revision: the expected yield has been lowered to 3.0 tonnes per acre for this season, replacing 3.5.

question: What is the total tonnage from the wheat block, and what are the net proceeds after the harvest and haulage deduction?
must_change: The total tonnage from the wheat block is 420 tonnes.
The net proceeds after the harvest and haulage deduction are $79,000.
must_persist: Wheat sells at $200 per tonne under the estate's contract.
The fixed harvest and haulage cost is $5,000.

sources.text: Axlevine Systems is finalizing a hardware order and applying a negotiated volume discount.
The hardware subtotal before any discount is $9,000.
A volume discount of 15 percent applies to the hardware subtotal.
A fixed shipping charge of $400 is added after the discount is applied.
Procurement wants the discount amount and the net payable including shipping.

assumption_A.text: A volume discount of 15 percent applies to the hardware subtotal.

not_A_evidence.text: Contract correction: the volume discount is 5 percent, not 15 percent; the higher rate was applied in error.

question: What is the discount amount on the hardware subtotal, and what is the net payable including shipping?
must_change: The discount amount on the hardware subtotal is $1,350.
The net payable including shipping is $8,050.
must_persist: The hardware subtotal before any discount is $9,000.
The fixed shipping charge is $400.

sources.text: Skylane Cargo is pricing an air-freight shipment of 600 kilograms.
The chargeable air rate is $4.50 per kilogram.
A fixed handling fee of $300 applies to the shipment.
A fixed security screening fee of $120 also applies to the shipment.
The shipper wants the weight-based air charge and the all-in shipment price including handling and screening.

assumption_A.text: The chargeable air rate is $4.50 per kilogram.

not_A_evidence.text: Rate update: the chargeable air rate is now $5.30 per kilogram, superseding the $4.50 figure.

question: What is the weight-based air charge, and what is the all-in shipment price including handling and screening?
must_change: The weight-based air charge is $2,700.
The all-in shipment price including handling and screening is $3,120.
must_persist: The fixed handling fee for the shipment is $300.
The fixed security screening fee for the shipment is $120.

sources.text: The Meadowlark Initiative is budgeting a micro-grant round for 25 recipients.
Each recipient receives a $400 stipend.
A fixed evaluation cost of $1,500 covers the review panel for the round.
A fixed reporting cost of $800 covers the round's final report.
The committee wants the total stipend outlay and the full round budget including evaluation and reporting.

assumption_A.text: Each recipient receives a $400 stipend.

not_A_evidence.text: Budget revision: each recipient's stipend has been raised to $480, replacing the $400 figure.

question: What is the total stipend outlay, and what is the full round budget including evaluation and reporting?
must_change: The total stipend outlay is $10,000.
The full round budget including evaluation and reporting is $12,300.
must_persist: The fixed evaluation cost for the round is $1,500.
The fixed reporting cost for the round is $800.

sources.text: Tidewell Apparel is costing a production batch that consumes 1,200 meters of fabric.
Fabric is purchased at $3.50 per meter.
Thread for the batch is a fixed $200 and cutting-room labor is a fixed $1,500, both independent of fabric price.
Production wants the fabric material cost and the total batch cost including thread and labor.

assumption_A.text: Fabric is purchased at $3.50 per meter.

not_A_evidence.text: Supplier update: fabric is now $4.00 per meter, replacing the $3.50 price.

question: What is the fabric material cost for the batch, and what is the total batch cost including thread and labor?
must_change: The fabric material cost for the batch is $4,200.
The total batch cost including thread and labor is $5,900.
must_persist: Thread for the batch is a fixed $200.
Cutting-room labor for the batch is a fixed $1,500.

sources.text: Clearbrook Dairy sells raw milk from two herds at a single contracted price per liter.
The north herd yields 4,000 liters this week and the south herd yields 2,500 liters this week.
The contracted price is $0.90 per liter, the same for both herds.
Accounting wants the north-herd revenue and the south-herd revenue for the week.

assumption_A.text: The contracted price is $0.90 per liter, the same for both herds.

not_A_evidence.text: Contract update: the milk price is now $1.10 per liter, replacing the $0.90 figure.

question: What is the north-herd revenue and the south-herd revenue for the week?
must_change: The north-herd revenue for the week is $3,600.
The south-herd revenue for the week is $2,250.
must_persist: The north herd yields 4,000 liters this week.
The south herd yields 2,500 liters this week.

sources.text: Meridian Print is quoting a run of 3,000 posters, with fixed design and shipping charges added separately.
Each poster costs $0.40 to print. Design is a fixed $350 and shipping is a fixed $250, both independent of the per-poster rate.
The client wants the poster print cost and the total quoted cost including design and shipping.

assumption_A.text: Each poster costs $0.40 to print.

not_A_evidence.text: Quote correction: each poster costs $0.55 to print, not $0.40; use the corrected rate.

question: What is the poster print cost, and what is the total quoted cost including design and shipping?
must_change: The poster print cost is $1,200.
The total quoted cost including design and shipping is $1,800.
must_persist: Design for the run is a fixed $350.
Shipping for the run is a fixed $250.

sources.text: Saffron Bistro is costing a service for 240 covers, with protein, produce, and dry goods tracked separately.
The protein cost is $4.75 per portion, one portion per cover.
Produce for the service is a fixed $600 and dry goods are a fixed $300, both independent of the protein price.
The chef wants the protein cost for the service and the total food cost including produce and dry goods.

assumption_A.text: The protein cost is $4.75 per portion, one portion per cover.

not_A_evidence.text: Supplier update: the protein cost is now $5.50 per portion, replacing the $4.75 figure.

question: What is the protein cost for the service, and what is the total food cost including produce and dry goods?
must_change: The protein cost for the service is $1,140.
The total food cost including produce and dry goods is $2,040.
must_persist: Produce for the service is a fixed $600.
Dry goods for the service are a fixed $300.

sources.text: Vanguard Couriers insures two depots' delivery vans at a single per-van annual premium.
The north depot operates 30 vans and the south depot operates 12 vans.
The annual premium is $420 per van, the same at both depots.
Finance wants the north-depot premium total and the south-depot premium total for the year.

assumption_A.text: The annual premium is $420 per van, the same at both depots.

not_A_evidence.text: Policy update: the annual premium is now $500 per van, replacing the $420 figure.

question: What is the north-depot premium total and the south-depot premium total for the year?
must_change: The north-depot premium total for the year is $12,600.
The south-depot premium total for the year is $5,040.
must_persist: The north depot operates 30 vans.
The south depot operates 12 vans.

sources.text: Voltmere operates a public EV charging site and bills a fleet customer for 4,000 kilowatt-hours in a month, plus fixed service charges.
Energy is billed at $0.16 per kilowatt-hour.
A network access charge is a fixed $200 per month.
A maintenance charge is a fixed $150 per month.
A connection charge is a fixed $90 per month.
The fleet customer wants the energy charge and the total monthly bill including network, maintenance, and connection.

assumption_A.text: Energy is billed at $0.16 per kilowatt-hour.

not_A_evidence.text: Tariff update: energy is now billed at $0.22 per kilowatt-hour, replacing the $0.16 rate.

question: What is the energy charge, and what is the total monthly bill including network, maintenance, and connection?
must_change: The energy charge is $640.
The total monthly bill including network, maintenance, and connection is $1,080.
must_persist: The network access charge is a fixed $200 per month.
The maintenance charge is a fixed $150 per month.

sources.text: Lantern Films is budgeting a shoot that requires 40 crew-days of labor, plus fixed equipment, location, and catering costs.
The crew day rate is $350 per crew-day.
Equipment rental for the shoot is a fixed $8,000.
Location fees for the shoot are a fixed $3,000.
Catering for the shoot is a fixed $2,000.
The producer wants the crew labor cost and the total shoot budget including equipment, location, and catering.

assumption_A.text: The crew day rate is $350 per crew-day.

not_A_evidence.text: Rate update: the crew day rate is now $420 per crew-day, replacing the $350 figure.

question: What is the crew labor cost, and what is the total shoot budget including equipment, location, and catering?
must_change: The crew labor cost is $14,000.
The total shoot budget including equipment, location, and catering is $27,000.
must_persist: Equipment rental for the shoot is a fixed $8,000.
Catering for the shoot is a fixed $2,000.

sources.text: Central Deck operates a 300-space parking structure and reports monthly operating results.
Monthly parking revenue is a fixed $36,000.
Per-space monthly maintenance cost is $8 per space.
Security is a fixed $4,000 per month, independent of the per-space maintenance rate.
The ground lease is a fixed $9,000 per month, independent of the per-space maintenance rate.
Management wants the total monthly operating cost and the net operating income for the month.

assumption_A.text: Per-space monthly maintenance cost is $8 per space.

not_A_evidence.text: Cost revision: per-space monthly maintenance cost is now $12 per space, replacing the $8 figure.

question: What is the total monthly operating cost, and what is the net operating income for the month?
must_change: The total monthly operating cost is $15,400.
The net operating income for the month is $20,600.
must_persist: Monthly parking revenue is a fixed $36,000.
Security is a fixed $4,000 per month.
